SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.networkconnection
Interface SdpPortManagerEvent

All Superinterfaces:
MediaEvent<SdpPortManager>, ResourceEvent<SdpPortManager>
public interface SdpPortManagerEvent
extends ResourceEvent<SdpPortManager>

An event from a SdpPortManager.

The EventType indicates which operation completed.

The Qualifier may return additional information about how/why the operation terminated, else it is set to ResourceEvent.NO_QUALIFIER.
More specific Qualifiers are listed below.

Field Summary
static EventType ANSWER_GENERATED
          EventType sent by a SdpPortManager when SdpPortManager.processSdpOffer(byte[]) has completed.
static EventType ANSWER_PROCESSED
          EventType sent by a SdpPortManager when SdpPortManager.processSdpAnswer(byte[]) has completed.
static EventType NETWORK_STREAM_FAILURE
          Event sent by the media server to indicate a hard failure on a media stream.
static EventType OFFER_GENERATED
          EventType sent by a SdpPortManager when SdpPortManager.generateSdpOffer() has completed.
static Qualifier OFFER_PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED
          ResourceEvent.getQualifier() returns this Qualifier to indicate that the offer is partially accepted (at least 1 Media description is accepted)
The application must use SdpPortManager.getMediaServerSessionDescription() to know what was accepted and what was rejected.
static Qualifier RESOURCE_PARTIALLY_AVAILABLE
          ResourceEvent.getQualifier() returns this Qualifier to indicate that a media resource cannot be allocated from the Media Server.
static MediaErr RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE
          Error sent by media server in case of resource shortage.
static MediaErr SDP_GLARE
          Error reported by the media server when both sides of the media channel attempted to re-negotiate the SDP at the same time.
static MediaErr SDP_NOT_ACCEPTABLE
          Error sent when the offer/answer procedure does not find any matching codec for any stream.
static EventType UNSOLICITED_OFFER_GENERATED
          Event sent by the media server to indicate that it requests a change in the local SDP.
 
Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.ResourceEvent
MANUAL_TRIGGER, NO_QUALIFIER, RTC_TRIGGERED, STOPPED
 
Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEvent
NO_ERROR
 
Method Summary
 byte[] getMediaServerSdp()
          Returns the most recent Media Server Session Description.
 
Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.ResourceEvent
getQualifier, getRTCTrigger
 
Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEvent
getError, getErrorText, getEventType, getSource, isSuccessful
 

Field Detail

OFFER_GENERATED

static final EventType OFFER_GENERATED
EventType sent by a SdpPortManager when SdpPortManager.generateSdpOffer() has completed.

ANSWER_GENERATED

static final EventType ANSWER_GENERATED
EventType sent by a SdpPortManager when SdpPortManager.processSdpOffer(byte[]) has completed.

ANSWER_PROCESSED

static final EventType ANSWER_PROCESSED
EventType sent by a SdpPortManager when SdpPortManager.processSdpAnswer(byte[]) has completed.

UNSOLICITED_OFFER_GENERATED

static final EventType UNSOLICITED_OFFER_GENERATED
Event sent by the media server to indicate that it requests a change in the local SDP.
(for example, IP address migration or other replication/high-availability feature)
Note that this is an unsolicited event (not caused by any API call).

NETWORK_STREAM_FAILURE

static final EventType NETWORK_STREAM_FAILURE
Event sent by the media server to indicate a hard failure on a media stream.
As a result, the stream has been teared down.
The media server SDP, as returned by getMediaServerSdp(), provides the list of remaining streams (possibly empty).
Note that this is an unsolicited event (not caused by any API call).

OFFER_PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED

static final Qualifier OFFER_PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED
ResourceEvent.getQualifier() returns this Qualifier to indicate that the offer is partially accepted (at least 1 Media description is accepted)
The application must use SdpPortManager.getMediaServerSessionDescription() to know what was accepted and what was rejected.

RESOURCE_PARTIALLY_AVAILABLE

static final Qualifier RESOURCE_PARTIALLY_AVAILABLE
ResourceEvent.getQualifier() returns this Qualifier to indicate that a media resource cannot be allocated from the Media Server. (but at least one Media description is properly allocated)
The application must use SdpPortManager.getMediaServerSessionDescription() to know more.

SDP_NOT_ACCEPTABLE

static final MediaErr SDP_NOT_ACCEPTABLE
Error sent when the offer/answer procedure does not find any matching codec for any stream.
This event should trigger a negative signaling answer ( >2xx ).
SdpPortManager.getMediaServerSessionDescription() returns null.

RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE

static final MediaErr RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE
Error sent by media server in case of resource shortage.
This event should trigger a negative signaling answer ( >2xx ).
SdpPortManager.getMediaServerSessionDescription() return null

SDP_GLARE

static final MediaErr SDP_GLARE
Error reported by the media server when both sides of the media channel attempted to re-negotiate the SDP at the same time.
If the signaling is SIP, it means that both sides sent a re-INVITE at the same time. See RFC 3261 for details about how to manage this.

Method Detail

getMediaServerSdp

byte[] getMediaServerSdp()
Returns the most recent Media Server Session Description.

Note that this Session Description may be not applied yet, if the negotiation is not completed, for example after a call to SdpPortManager.generateSdpOffer().
